frameworks.name: Bayesian switching dynamical systems
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian algorithm infers hidden brain states and their transition dynamics from time-series data

frameworks.name: hidden brain state dynamics
frameworks.one_line: latent transient states reconfigure functional circuits during cognition

frameworks.name: task-condition driven brain activity
frameworks.one_line: framework assuming brain states track external task demands tightly

core_question: How do hidden, time-varying brain states regulate cognitive performance and decision-making dynamics?
key_assumptions: brain activity reflects latent states beyond external task conditions
states are discrete and have inferable transition probabilities
functional circuits can be reconfigured by dynamic state transitions
manipulations: comparison of inferred hidden states against external task conditions
replication across a large independent sample
key_findings: hidden brain states are only weakly aligned with external task conditions
state transitions support flexible reconfiguration of functional circuits
specific transient states are optimal for cognition
failure to engage optimal states in time predicts poorer performance and weaker decision dynamics
interpretations: cognition depends on flexible latent brain state dynamics rather than task-locked activity
timely engagement of optimal hidden states underlies effective decision-making
